emails.body:
NEXTGOV/FCW TECH UPDATE — October 2, 2026

TOP STORIES

• GSA launches identity federation pilot across three civilian agencies
  The General Services Administration began a 90-day pilot testing a shared identity credential framework at Commerce, Energy, and HHS. The effort aims to reduce redundant PIV card issuance for contractors moving between agencies. Early metrics show 40% reduction in onboarding time.

• CISA releases updated software supply chain guidance (v2.1)
  New attestation requirements for critical software vendors take effect January 2027. Key changes: mandatory SBOM delivery in SPDX 3.0 format, automated vulnerability disclosure pipelines, and third-party penetration testing for high-impact components. Public comment period closes November 15.

• NIST seeks feedback on post-quantum migration playbook
  Draft NIST SP 800-208 revision addresses hybrid cryptographic transitions for federal systems. Timeline targets CNSA 2.0 alignment by FY2029. Comments due November 30 via regulations.gov.

QUICK HITS

- DoD CIO issues memo on zero-trust architecture maturity model assessments due Q2 FY27
- OMB M-24-10 implementation guidance updated for AI inventory reporting
- Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) adds 12 new authorized services

UPCOMING EVENTS

- Oct 15: ACT-IAC Cybersecurity Summit (virtual)
- Oct 22-23: FedRAMP PMO Office Hours
- Nov 5: NIST PQC Migration Workshop

---

NEXTGOV/FCW TECH UPDATE — October 5, 2026

TOP STORIES

• Data center capacity crunch drives new federal siting guidance
  OMB and DOE joint memo directs agencies to prioritize colocation and shared infrastructure for new HPC workloads. Northern Virginia, Phoenix, and Columbus corridors flagged for power constraints through 2028. Agencies must submit 5-year capacity plans by December 31.

• Cloud smart strategy refresh emphasizes workload portability
  Updated OMB guidance pushes multi-cloud architectures for mission-critical systems. New requirements: quarterly portability testing, standardized API contracts, and egress cost tracking. FedRAMP High baseline updated to include cross-cloud identity sync.

• Model governance framework advances toward government-wide standard
  NIST AI RMF 2.0 draft adds model card requirements, red-team testing cadence, and continuous monitoring thresholds for deployed systems. DoD CDAO piloting at three combatant commands. Public workshop scheduled October 28.

QUICK HITS

- GSA announces $2.3B blanket purchase agreement for edge computing infrastructure
- CISA KEV catalog adds 14 vulnerabilities, including two actively exploited in federal networks
- Senate Homeland Security Committee schedules markup on Federal AI Accountability Act
- Treasury issues guidance on digital identity verification for benefit programs

RESEARCH & REPORTS

- GAO-26-106XXX: Cloud Concentration Risk in Federal Civilian Agencies
- CRS R47XXX: Data Center Energy Demand Projections Through 2035
- NIST IR 85XX: Model Governance for High-Impact AI Systems

UPCOMING EVENTS

- Oct 18: GSA Edge Computing Industry Day
- Oct 28: NIST AI RMF 2.0 Public Workshop (hybrid)
- Nov 4-6: AWS GovCloud Summit, Washington DC
- Nov 12: Federal Zero Trust Working Group Meeting
